name: edge-functions
context: Supabase Edge Functions — deploy Deno-based TypeScript functions for server-side logic, webhooks, and API endpoints
info: Supabase Edge Functions are server-side TypeScript/JavaScript functions that run on Deno Deploy. Each function is an HTTP handler in `supabase/functions/<name>/index.ts` using the Web Standard `Request`/`Response` API. Functions have access to environment variables (secrets), can import npm packages via `npm:` specifiers, and can use the Supabase client with the service role key for admin operations. Deploy via `supabase functions deploy <name>`. Invoke from the client SDK via `supabase.functions.invoke('name', { body })` or directly via HTTP POST to `https://<ref>.supabase.co/functions/v1/<name>`. Edge Functions are ideal for: webhooks, third-party API integrations, Stripe payment processing, OpenAI/LLM calls, email sending, and any logic that shouldn't run on the client.

sections.title: Create and deploy
sections.body: `supabase functions new <name>` creates `supabase/functions/<name>/index.ts`. Write a `Deno.serve()` handler using Web Standard Request/Response. `supabase functions serve` for local dev. `supabase functions deploy <name>` to deploy. Each function gets its own URL: `https://<ref>.supabase.co/functions/v1/<name>`.

sections.title: Invoking functions
sections.body: From the SDK: `supabase.functions.invoke('name', { body })`. Via HTTP: POST to the function URL with `Authorization: Bearer <key>` header. The anon key is passed automatically by the SDK. For unauthenticated access, set `--no-verify-jwt` when deploying. Functions receive the caller's JWT in the Authorization header.

sections.title: Secrets and environment
sections.body: `supabase secrets set KEY=value` stores encrypted env vars for functions. `SUPABASE_URL`, `SUPABASE_ANON_KEY`, `SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY`, and `SUPABASE_DB_URL` are auto-injected. Access with `Deno.env.get('KEY')`. For local dev, use `--env-file .env.local` with `supabase functions serve`.

sections.title: Common patterns
sections.body: **Webhook handler**: Verify signature, parse event, process. **API proxy**: Call third-party APIs with server-side secrets. **Auth guard**: Extract JWT from Authorization header, call `supabase.auth.getUser()`. **Admin operations**: Use service role client to bypass RLS. **CORS**: Handle OPTIONS preflight and set Access-Control headers.
